HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT LEAR SIEGLER DIVERSIFIED HOLDINGS CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT AND ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S NEGLIGENCE AND STRICT LIABILITY CAUSES OF ACTION ARE DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS INITIAL BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. BY FAILING TO DEMONSTRATE WITH ITS MOVING PAPERS THAT IT PROPOUNDED COMPREHENSIVE INTERROGATORIES DESIGNED TO ELICIT ALL THE EVIDENCE PLAINTIFF HAS TO SUPPORT HIS CONTENTION OF LIABILITY AND BY FAILING TO SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY DEMONSTRATES THAT HE HAS NO KNOWLEDGE TO SUPPORT HIS CONTENTION OF LIABILITY, DEFENDANT IS PRECLUDED FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. (SEE WEBER V. JOHN CRANE, INC. (2007) 143 CAL.APP.4TH 1433; SCHEIDING V. DINWIDDIE CONST. CO. (1999) 69 CAL.APP.4TH 64.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. RULING:
DEFENDANT LEAR SIEGLER DIVERSIFIED HOLDINGS CORP.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Lear Siegler Diversified Holdings Corp.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's negligence and strict liability causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to demonstrate with its moving papers that it propounded comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiff has to support his contention of liability and by failing to show that plaintiff's deposition testimony demonstrates that he has no knowledge to support his contention of liability, defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. (See Weber v. John Crane, Inc. (2007) 143 Cal.App.4th 1433; Scheiding v. Dinwiddie Const. Co. (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 64.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Patricia Rodriguez of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
